diagrams.title: Basic Class Inheritance Hierarchy
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a parent class 'Animal' and its child classes 'Dog' and 'Cat' showing the inheritance relationship. Arrows point from child to parent indicating they inherit properties and methods. This diagram helps learners understand the IS-A relationship and how code reuse works through inheritance.
elements: Animal class rectangle (top, parent)
Dog class rectangle (bottom-left, child)
Cat class rectangle (bottom-right, child)
Inheritance arrows pointing from Dog and Cat to Animal
Shared methods displayed inside Animal: speak(), eat()
Unique methods in Dog: fetch()
Unique methods in Cat: climb()
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental concept of inheritance where child classes inherit attributes and methods from parent classes, demonstrating code reuse and the IS-A relationship

diagrams.title: Multiple Inheritance in Python
diagrams.description: A diagram showing a child class 'FlyingCar' that inherits from multiple parent classes 'Car' and 'Airplane'. This illustrates Python's support for multiple inheritance and how a class can combine features from multiple sources.
elements: Car class rectangle (left, parent)
Airplane class rectangle (right, parent)
FlyingCar class rectangle (bottom, child with two parents)
Two inheritance arrows pointing from FlyingCar to both parents
Methods in Car: drive()
Methods in Airplane: fly()
Methods in FlyingCar: both drive() and fly()
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates Python's multiple inheritance capability, showing how a class can inherit from more than one parent class to combine functionality

diagrams.title: Method Override and Super() Function
diagrams.description: A diagram showing method overriding where a child class 'Dog' overrides the 'speak()' method from parent 'Animal', and uses super() to call the parent constructor. Visualizes the flow of method calls and the concept of polymorphism.
elements: Animal class box with __init__ and speak() method
Dog class box with overridden __init__ using super() and overridden speak()
Arrow showing super().__init__() call from Dog to Animal
Arrow showing method override concept
Code snippet boxes showing the actual Python syntax
Output example: Animal says 'Sound' vs Dog says 'Woof'
diagrams.purpose: Explains method overriding and the super() function, showing how child classes can customize inherited behavior while still leveraging parent class functionality
